title: Teaching
text: Since 2022, I have taught undergraduate (Years 1–3) and graduate courses (Master’s level) to diverse audiences (economics, engineering, and environmental studies students), in lectures, tutorials, and computer-based classes (over 330 teaching hours in total). My teaching covers both core economics courses and more specialized topics, with particular attention to socio-­ecological issues and transitions. I combine theoretical foundations, empirical analysis, and critical discussion of public policies, while promoting a pluralist and reflexive approach to economics.

title: Clémence Dedinger
text:
Email
     CV (in French)
     Scholar
  

  
    
    
      Clémence Dedinger
      Postdoctoral Researcher
      
        I am a postdoctoral researcher at CRIEG‑REGARDS (University of Reims Champagne‑Ardenne) in social‑ecological economics. I lead a research project funded by the Junior Professorship Chair “Economics of Ecological Transition and Bioeconomy” on the governance of renaturation in North Karelia (Finland). More broadly, I analyse socio‑ecological transitions  with a focus on governance, institutional change, and environmental justice.
      
    
  






  
  Makkaralatva-aapa peatland, © T. Mustonen

title: Science & Society
text: I consider engaging with broader audiences and contributing to public discussions an integral part of my work as a researcher. Beyond academic publications, I develop collaborations across disciplines and participate in exhibitions and projects at the intersection of science, art, and society, exploring alternative ways of producing and sharing knowledge.

title: Science & Society
text:
Selected initiatives


 
  
  

  
    Coastal marsh heritage in the Fier d’Ars
    
      Public exhibition presenting the results of the ANR PAMPAS project (2019–2023), exploring changes in the heritage identity of the marshes of the Pertuis Charentais in response to marine flooding over the past half-century. Held at the Ecomuseum of Loix-en-Ré (Île de Ré). 
      Read more 
    
  
  




 

© Olivier Crouzel
  

  
    Dikes and People
    
      Arts and science project combining geography and visual art.
The installation brings together scientific analysis and artistic creation to offer a sensitive understanding of human–nature relationships in coastal marsh landscapes. My own fieldwork interviews were used as source material for the project. 
      Read more 
    
  
  



  

  
    Newsletter of the Atlantic Marshes
    
    The Forum des Marais Atlantiques publishes a newsletter that reflects day-to-day initiatives across coastal wetlands. I contributed by sharing my research findings in this publication.
